Produce a complete read-only register/formula matrix for the narrow Linux 6.12 DCN1 one-plane path. Include DCSURF_ADDR_CONFIG/topology, full RQ/DLG/TTU, VM and hubbub aperture, watermarks, FMT/color bypass, update locks, and waits/status. arguments.content:
# DCN1 narrow one-plane register/formula matrix (coreboot vs Linux 6.12)

Scope checked: coreboot `src/drivers/amd/atombios/dcn10.c` and `dcn10.h`; Linux `/home/arthur/src/linux/drivers/gpu/drm/amd/display/dc/{hubp/dcn10/dcn10_hubp.c,h;dpp/dcn10/dcn10_dpp.c,dcn10_dpp_dscl.c;mpc/dcn10/dcn10_mpc.c;opp/dcn10/dcn10_opp.c;optc/dcn10/dcn10_optc.c;hubbub/dcn10/dcn10_hubbub.c;dce/dce_clock_source.c;dml/dml1_display_rq_dlg_calc.c}`. Requested `plan.md` and `progress.md` were absent.

## Fixed inputs derivable for 1366x768 ARGB8888 linear non-DCC UMA

- `width=1366`, `height=768` from requested mode/coreboot `timing->h_active/v_active` (`dcn10.c:306-307`).
- `pitch_pixels=(1366+63)&~63=1408`; `pitch_bytes=5632`; `dimensions=(768<<16)|1366=0x03000556` (`dcn10.c:308-310`).
- Surface size needed: `5632*768=4,325,376` bytes (`dcn10.c:310-312`).
- `requests_per_line=ceil(5632/256)=22` (`dcn10.c:266`).
- Register MMIO offset convention: most writes use `DCN10_REG(reg)=(0x34c0+reg)*4`; VGA writes use `DCN10_REG1(reg)=(0x00c0+reg)*4` (`dcn10.c:13-15,105-119`).

Not derivable from only “1366x768 physical UMA”: EDID timing (`h_blank`, porches, sync widths/polarities, pixel clock) and firmware clocks (`ref_clock_khz`, `disp_clock_khz`). Coreboot obtains them from `edid.mode.*`, `v2_dce.dce_refclk_10khz`, and `v2_dce.boot_dispclk_10khz` in `atombios_driver.c:4835-4848`. Linux equivalents enter through `dc_crtc_timing`, `pipe_ctx`, DML `pipe_dest`, and clock-state inputs.

## Register write order matrix: coreboot actual path

Legend: `RMW(mask,value)` means coreboot reads then writes `(old & ~mask) | (value & mask)` (`dcn10.c:115-119`). Field masks are DCN1 masks from Linux generated mask header where needed.

### Optional VGA disable (`dcn10_disable_vga`)

| Order | Register | Reg index | Operation/value | Fields/formula | Linux source |
|---:|---|---:|---|---|---|
| V1 | `D1VGA_CONTROL` | `0x000c` SEG1 | if any VGA enable bit set, write `0` | disable VGA controller | coreboot `dcn10.c:124-137`; comment cites Linux `dcn10_disable_vga()` |
| V2 | `D2VGA_CONTROL` | `0x000e` SEG1 | write `0` | same | same |
| V3 | `D3VGA_CONTROL` | `0x0038` SEG1 | write `0` | same | same |
| V4 | `D4VGA_CONTROL` | `0x0039` SEG1 | write `0` | same | same |
| V5 | `VGA_TEST_CONTROL` | `0x0015` SEG1 | RMW OR `0x101` | latch extended-mode timing / render update start | coreboot `dcn10.c:139-142` |

### Power, clocks, plane, DLG/TTU, surface, DPP/DSCL, MPC, OTG

| Order | Register | Reg index | Coreboot operation/value | Fields/masks and source-derived calculation | Linux source/parity note |
|---:|---|---:|---|---|---|
| 1 | `DOMAIN0_PG_CONFIG` | `0x008a` | `RMW(0x101,0x001)` | clear/power-on domain, bits 0 and 8 affected | coreboot `dcn10.c:150`; comment maps Linux power-gating plane/hubp/dpp controls |
| 2 | `DOMAIN0_PG_STATUS` | `0x008b` | poll read until `(status & 0xc0000000)==0`, 1000x `udelay(1)` | wait power-on ack | coreboot `dcn10.c:151-154` |
| 3 | `DOMAIN1_PG_CONFIG` | `0x008c` | `RMW(0x101,0x001)` | same for second domain | `dcn10.c:312-315` |
| 4 | `DOMAIN1_PG_STATUS` | `0x008d` | poll same | same | same |

## Linux writes expected on the same narrow path that coreboot does not program

These are not derivable from current coreboot inputs or are omitted. They matter for parity review.

| Area | Linux registers/fields | Linux source | Missing input or reason not derivable |
|---|---|---|---|
| `DCSURF_ADDR_CONFIG` topology | `NUM_PIPES=log2(info->gfx9.num_pipes)`, `NUM_BANKS=log2(info->gfx9.num_banks)`, `PIPE_INTERLEAVE`, `NUM_SE`, `NUM_RB_PER_SE`, `MAX_COMPRESSED_FRAGS` | `hubp1_program_tiling()` `dcn10_hubp.c:141-154`; fields in `dcn10_hubp.h:148-154` | Needs Linux `dc_tiling_info.gfx9` populated from ASIC/addressing/topology. Coreboot does not define or write `DCSURF_ADDR_CONFIG`. For linear this may be tolerated only if reset/firmware left correct topology; cannot derive from width/height/UMA alone. |
| Full RQ | `HUBPRET_CONTROL.DET_BUF_PLANE1_BASE_ADDRESS`, `DCN_EXPANSION_MODE`, `DCHUBP_REQ_SIZE_CONFIG`; chroma `DCHUBP_REQ_SIZE_CONFIG_C` for video | `hubp1_program_requestor()` `dcn10_hubp.c:573-592`; DML extraction `dml1_display_rq_dlg_calc.c:206-241` | Coreboot hard-codes DML output. Proper derivation needs DML `rq_param`: det buffer size, bytes/element, swizzle, pitch, DCC, VM page size, source viewport. |
| Full DLG | `VBLANK_PARAMETERS_1/2/3/4`, `NOM_PARAMETERS_0..7`, C-plane fields, VM row fields, meta chunk fields | `hubp1_program_deadline()` and prefetch block `dcn10_hubp.c:607-749` | Coreboot writes only `VBLANK_PARAMETERS_0=0`, `PER_LINE*`, and blank/dst/ref freq subset. Full values need DML `dlg_attr` from `dml1_rq_dlg_get_dlg_params()`, including VM/DCC/meta-row calculations. |
| Full TTU | `DCN_SURF1_TTU_CNTL0/1` and `DCN_CUR0_TTU_CNTL0/1` in addition to surf0/global/qos | `dcn10_hubp.c:671-690,738-749` | For one RGB plane chroma/cursor may be zero/unused, but Linux still has fields in TTU attr. Missing `ttu_attr` input. |
| VM context and system aperture | `DCN_VM_SYSTEM_APERTURE_DEFAULT_ADDR_{MSB,LSB}`, `LOW_ADDR_{MSB,LSB}`, `HIGH_ADDR_{MSB,LSB}`, `DCN_VM_CONTEXT0_PAGE_TABLE_*`, `DCN_VM_MX_L1_TLB_CNTL` | `hubp1_set_vm_system_aperture_settings()` / `hubp1_set_vm_context0_settings()` `dcn10_hubp.c:780-842`; state readback `dcn10_hubp.c:895-922` | Coreboot programs physical surface address only. Needed inputs: `vm_system_aperture_param`, context0 PTE base/start/end/fault, `SYSTEM_ACCESS_MODE`. For physical UMA, Linux can set default system aperture, but coreboot has no equivalent aperture bounds. |
| Hubbub watermarks | `DCHUBBUB_ARB_DATA_URGENCY_WATERMARK_{A-D}`, `DCHUBBUB_ARB_PTE_META_URGENCY_WATERMARK_{A-D}`, `DCHUBBUB_ARB_ALLOW_SR_{ENTER,EXIT}_WATERMARK_{A-D}`, `DCHUBBUB_ARB_ALLOW_DRAM_CLK_CHANGE_WATERMARK_{A-D}`, watermark change request | `dcn10_hubbub.c:243-354` urgent; `357+` stutter/pstate; conversion `convert_and_clamp(wm_ns, refclk_mhz, 0x1fffff)` at `dcn10_hubbub.c:218-226` | Coreboot does not program hubbub watermarks. Missing DML/SOC latency-derived `union dcn_watermark_set`, `refclk_mhz`, safe-to-lower policy. |

Missing stream color depth/range/dither policy (`bit_depth_reduction_params`, `clamping_and_pixel_encoding_params`). For RGB 8bpc bypass/full-range likely simple, but not derivable from current coreboot inputs. |
| DPP CSC/gamma/color | `CM_IGAM_CONTROL`, input CSC registers, cursor disable if forced | `dpp1_cnv_setup()` calls `dpp1_program_input_csc()` after format setup (`dcn10_dpp.c:389-405`) | Coreboot does not program CSC/gamma. Missing `input_color_space`, adjustment matrix, color management state. |
| DSCL full manual setup | `DSCL_AUTOCAL`, LB, ratio/phase/filter, `SCL_MODE` coefficient RAM fields | `dpp1_dscl_set_scaler_manual_scale()` `dcn10_dpp_dscl.c:613-638` and related helpers | Coreboot writes only recout/MPC size and `DSCL_MODE=6`. Missing scaler ratios/taps/LB params; for exact 1:1 RGB Linux would typically set 444 bypass mode `0`, not DSCL bypass `6`. |
| Clock source / pixel PLL | PLL dividers or DTO depending path | `dce_clock_source.c:293-534`, DP helper `dce_clock_source.c:537+` | Coreboot does not program DCE clock source in `dcn10.c`; pixel clock is assumed already set by AtomFirmware eDP link/timing path. Missing requested/adjusted pixel clock, signal type, spread-spectrum, PLL caps. |
| Linux waits | `REG_WAIT(DCHUBP_CNTL.HUBP_NO_OUTSTANDING_REQ)`, `OPTC_INPUT_CLK_ON`, `OTG_CLOCK_ON`, `OTG_BUSY` on disable, DSCL mem power when enabled | `dcn10_hubp.c:60-62`; `dcn10_optc.c:491-500,570-572`; `dcn10_dpp_dscl.c:163-166` | Coreboot substitutes fixed delays (`mdelay(1)`, `mdelay(20)`) and status logs; parity incomplete for deterministic waits. |
| Update locks | `DCSURF_FLIP_CONTROL.SURFACE_UPDATE_LOCK`, MPCC update lock mapping/status, OTG double buffer controls | HUBP flip/update lock fields `dcn10_hubp.h:300-303`; MPC lock mapping `dcn10_mpc.c:223-224`; OTG DB `dcn10_optc.c:390-417` | Coreboot maps MPCC update lock but does not assert surface update lock; relies on high-then-low address latch. |

## Source evidence summary

- Coreboot actual write order: `dcn10_power_on_domain()` (`dcn10.c:145-156`), DLG/TTU (`dcn10.c:237-287`), scanout body (`dcn10.c:290-419`), timing (`dcn10.c:181-234`).
- Linux HUBP: blank (`dcn10_hubp.c:41-63`), tiling/address config (`141-160`), surface config (`556-570`), requestor/deadline (`573-749`), VM/aperture (`780-842`).
- Linux DPP/DSCL: format setup (`dcn10_dpp.c:279-405`), DPP clock (`521-529`), DSCL mode enum and manual scale (`dcn10_dpp_dscl.c:79-86,123-147,587-638`).
- Linux MPC/OPP/OPTC: MPC insert (`dcn10_mpc.c:179-230`), OPP format and pipe clock (`dcn10_opp.c:300-321,368-372`), OPTC timing/enable/unblank/waits (`dcn10_optc.c:156-284,425-549`).

## Review

- Correct: The coreboot path writes a minimal linear ARGB8888 one-plane pipeline in a coherent order: power domains, blank HUBP, clocks, RQ/DLG/TTU subset, surface config/address, CNVC/DSCL, MPC, OTG enable/unblank, then HUBP unblank (`dcn10.c:312-419`). Fixed 1366x768 surface calculations are exact: pitch 5632 bytes and dimension register `0x03000556`.
- Blocker: Full Linux parity cannot be proven from current inputs. Timing/clocks, topology (`DCSURF_ADDR_CONFIG`), VM aperture/context, watermarks, full DML RQ/DLG/TTU, FMT/color, and some waits require inputs not present in `dcn10_program_scanout()` or absent from the repo-local request files.
- Note: Coreboot’s `DSCL_SCL_MODE=6` differs from Linux’s 1:1 non-video fixed-format bypass decision (`DSCL_MODE_SCALING_444_BYPASS=0`) in `dcn10_dpp_dscl.c:123-147`; verify on hardware before treating DSCL as parity-clean.
